review_basis: DERIVED_FROM_INDEX_NOTES + DESIGN_SPECS + direct visual inspection of 14 captures (light + dark). The frontend-designer subagent confirmed scores via index.json cross-check; the orchestrator (this run) inspected every PNG directly.

findings.title: Map tiles do not paint on cold load for polyline routes — hero element blank
evidence: captures/light/polyline-01-top.png — only a single blue dot on a flat light-blue background; no road tiles visible
captures/dark/polyline-01-top.png — same symptom (single blue dot on near-black; no tiles)
findings.fix: Wait for onMapLoaded / onRegionIsChangingComplete before the first paint. Add a copper-tinted loading shimmer over the ~40% map zone until tiles paint. Verify whether this is a capture-timing race (Wasatch was opened first) or a real cold-start tile-fetch failure. Contrast: the Blue Ridge Overlook fixture (no polyline) renders tiles fine, so the SDK works.

findings.title: No copper polyline visible on Cherohala deep-link even though tiles render
evidence: captures/light/plan-03-detail.png — Atlantic / SE US tiles render correctly, but only a single blue dot is shown instead of a copper polyline tracing the route
findings.fix: Debug the DESIGN-003 AC-1 render path. Confirm (a) `cherohala-skyway` fixture's `routePolyline` is non-null, (b) the Polyline child is mounted with `stroke=copper-500 (#EE7C2B)`, `width=4`, and (c) the camera fits route bounds. Distinct root cause from F1 (here tiles DO paint).

findings.title: Geometry-degradation invariant violated on Cherohala (no polyline AND no 'Approximate location' badge)
evidence: captures/light/plan-03-detail.png — only a centroid dot is visible; no badge
findings.fix: Render the correct DESIGN-003 branch for the fixture. Add a maestro invariant: every detail screen shows exactly one of {polyline} | {centroid + 'Approximate location' badge} — never both, never neither.

findings.title: Centroid marker is the default blue user-location puck, not the copper pin (DESIGN-003 enrichment)
evidence: captures/light/plan-03-detail.png — blue dot in the middle of Atlantic
captures/light/no-polyline-01-top.png — but here the marker IS copper! Inconsistency.
DESIGN-003 §Design enrichment — copper centroid marker required
findings.fix: Render the copper centroid Marker asset at centroidLat/Lng. Disable `showsUserLocation` on detail map instances. Investigate why the no-polyline branch (Blue Ridge) renders the correct copper pin but polyline-bearing branches render the default SDK puck.

findings.title: Pull-to-refresh 'Refreshing...' banner is system blue — the only off-brand accent in the capture set
evidence: captures/light/plan-02-suggestions.png — solid blue banner at top
findings.fix: Theme the RN RefreshControl to copper-500 (#EE7C2B): pass `colors={[copper-500]}` and `tintColor={copper-500}`. Re-capture to confirm only copper accents remain across the home + refresh states.

findings.title: Save action never resolves to 'Saved' state in this run — known identifier reconciliation issue (FU-2 / DATA-006)
evidence: Save button tapped via maestro; waited 15s for `save-curated-saved-badge` — FAILED
Screenshot at failure: button still in copper 'Save' idle state

Source code (app/(app)/curated-route/[id].tsx:235-238): `useSaveCuratedRoute({ curatedRouteId: detail?._id ?? '', ... })` — relies on `_id` from DATA-006, which is NOT returned by `getCuratedRouteDetail` per the in-source IDENTIFIER RECONCILIATION comment
findings.fix: Either (a) extend DATA-006's `getCuratedRouteDetail` return to include `_id` (per FU-2 the field IS in the validator but the React hook uses `detail._id` not `detail.routeId`), or (b) change the hook to thread `routeId` through to a new mutation that resolves `_id` server-side via `ctx.db.query('curated_routes').withIndex('by_routeId', ...).unique()` before insert. The gate run in `gate-results.json` reports step 5 PASSED, so this is a fresh regression or a state-dependent flakiness — investigate.

findings.title: Dark-theme coverage gap — 4 of 9 surfaces have no dark capture
evidence: captures/dark/ contains 5 files; light/ contains 9 — parity gap
findings.fix: Add dark variants for plan-home, plan-suggestions, plan-03-detail, save-idle in the next capture pass. Use `xcrun simctl ui booted appearance dark` + relaunch.

findings.title: DESIGN-001 AC-1 spec self-contradicts on scenic fill (74% vs 90%)
findings.fix: Reconcile to `Math.round(score * 100)` — visually confirmed the Wasatch scenic bar reads 90% (matches the higher number), so the 74% reference is the stale one.

strengths: No-polyline branch (Blue Ridge Overlook) is the gold-standard reference: real tiles, copper centroid, italic 'No description yet' placeholder, 'Approximate location' badge, archetype badge, all five score bars, conditions row, Save + Ride It inside scroll.
Score-dimension bar primitive is well executed: copper-500 fill on near-black track, % readout right-aligned, sufficient row height, dark-mode parity is clean.
Brand identity (copper accent) comes through on the home/plan view: copper target compass, copper dashed route polylines, copper wrench, copper 'Drawing the map' pill.
Dark theme is genuinely considered — not just inverted: badge backgrounds, button outlines, and score-bar tracks all read correctly.
Info hierarchy on the detail screen is clean — the eye lands on the composite score, then the five bars, then the actions. The 40/60 map-vs-body split matches DESIGN-002.
